name: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #14
description: Florentine Groteschi #14 is a toxic emerald tabloid single-panel relic built around Raphael and Doomscroll Cathedral Noir. Tarocchi Fiorentini assigns Capricorno, used here as the witness card. A Fanum-tax indulgence slip from the wool guild gets pulled into a read-only wallet permission modal, making the panel read like a corrupted receipt for belief, status, and patronage. The collector gets a joke, but the joke has a ledger. The generated insert, a pixelated espresso cup that steams private keys, acts as the modern clue; Gematria marks LORE at Σ 300.

attributes.value: a pixelated espresso cup that steams private keys

florentine_groteschi.collector_note: Custom top-level lore data: not an OpenSea trait list, but intentionally readable in the raw token JSON.

chess_piece.name: Bishop

rarity.ranking_rule: Higher chess strength/rank is intentionally rarer: Pawn common, Knight/Bishop uncommon, Rook rare, Queen epic, King legendary.

board_position.position_rule: Deterministic per-token board square reserved for future game/use mechanics; independent of visible glyph placement.

chess_piece.allocation_rule: Weighted deterministic shuffled chess allocation by piece strength: {'Pawn': 560, 'Knight': 280, 'Bishop': 280, 'Rook': 168, 'Queen': 84, 'King': 29}
chess_piece.metadata_visibility: Hidden from OpenSea attributes; stored only in florentine_groteschi.hidden_traits.
chess_piece.visual_rule: A small unlabeled foreground chess glyph shows the chess-piece graphic with the piece name encoded underneath; that visible code is intentionally not serialized in metadata.

artwork_breakdown.generated_insert: a pixelated espresso cup that steams private keys

tarocchi_fiorentini.allocation_rule: 14 complete 97-card sets across 1,401 tokens, plus 43 deterministic extras

artwork_breakdown.composition_read: Raphael anchors the historical source. Capricorno supplies the witness logic. The pixelated espresso cup that steams private keys acts as the modern meme-object. The toxic emerald tabloid palette and Doomscroll Cathedral Noir styling make the panel read like a corrupted Renaissance case file.

groteschi_explanation.plain_english: Read plainly: Raphael is treated as evidence in a reliquary support ticket. The old-world clue is a Fanum-tax indulgence slip from the wool guild; the modern-world echo is a read-only wallet permission modal. The joke is that patronage, holy paperwork, and marketplace verification are all status systems asking who gets believed.
groteschi_explanation.read_between_the_lines: The Capricorno card is not decorative; it tells you how to read the room. The old clue and the modern clue compete as evidence, so the panel becomes a custody argument about belief, status, and receipts. The collector gets a joke, but the joke has a ledger. The slang turns sacred authority into aura, patronage into clout economics, and marginalia into groupchat evidence.

slang_for_normal_plebs.translation_for_normal_plebs: did it perfectly; in this collection a fresco, saint, or relic can 'eat' like a viral post

hidden_solution.method: Split the visible marker into DNA triplets, translate each triplet through the genetic-code table, then read the amino-acid letters as the source Base name. Non-standard letters use documented stop/nearest triplet placeholders.

hidden_acrostic.method: Read the first character of each acrostic line; slashes are spaces.
